text.title: Viral Loops and Types Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules every application of `Viral Loops and Types` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Pick exactly ONE primary loop archetype. Stacking 2+ primaries dilutes mechanic clarity. Allowed: 1 primary + 1 optional secondary supporting it.

rationale.source: Reforge / Casey Winters product loops curriculum

rule.rationale: Each archetype consumes UX surface and user attention. Stacking = users don't understand which loop they're in; both underperform.

rule.statement: Archetype MUST match product's natural shape. Content app → Content loop; collaboration tool → Inherent loop; commodity app → Incentivized loop; passion product → Word-of-Mouth loop.

rationale.source: Andrew Chen viral typology; Reforge frameworks

rule.rationale: Forcing Incentivized on a passion product cheapens; forcing Word-of-Mouth on a commodity tool produces silence. Match shape = mechanic feels native.

rule.statement: Target K-factor MUST be within archetype's empirical range: WoM 0.1-0.3, Inherent 0.3-0.6, Incentivized 0.2-0.5, Content 0.2-0.8, Outbreak 1.0+. Targeting K > 1 outside Outbreak is unrealistic.

rationale.source: Andrew Chen viral coefficient case studies; Sequoia growth corpus

rule.rationale: K > 1 sustained is extremely rare outside outbreak / network-effect products (Dropbox, Hotmail, TikTok). Targeting it in other archetypes burns budget on unattainable goal.

rule.statement: If using Incentivized archetype, reward MUST cost < 50% of blended CAC. Above 50%, viral loop becomes a more-expensive paid channel.
rule.rationale: Generous incentives create K-factor lift but unit economics break. Cap at 50% CAC; if loop needs more, archetype mismatched OR product not yet sticky enough.

rule.statement: Viral loop MUST run on a product with D30 retention >= 25% (consumer) / >= 40% (prosumer). Below threshold, viral inflow exits at same rate — economics negative.
rule.rationale: Viral on leaky-bucket = inflate user count without revenue. Retention is the multiplier; without it the loop is theatre.

rule.statement: When preconditions in Applies If fail OR any item in Skip If triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
